Cloning https://aur.archlinux.org/ttf-iosevka.git to /home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-1nr8kr5w...

Analyzing ttf-iosevka AUR package (working in /home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-1nr8kr5w) with qwen/qwen3-235b-a22b-2507 from https://openrouter.ai/api/v1
Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD defines standard sources: a zip archive and a LICENSE.md file, both fetched over HTTPS from GitHub domains associated with the official Iosevka project (github.com/be5invis/Iosevka and raw.githubusercontent.com). The URLs use the `pkgver` variable, which is statically defined as 34.6.3 and does not involve command substitution or dynamic fetching. The `sha256sums` are provided and match the sources, ensuring integrity.

During `makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare`, only the global scope and `pkgver()` (if present) are evaluated. This PKGBUILD has no `pkgver()` function, and the top-level content consists solely of variable assignments. No code is executed at parse time — there are no command substitutions, function calls, or shell executions in the global scope. The `source` array uses static URLs and standard HTTPS sources, which are safe to download for auditing.

The `package()` function is irrelevant here due to the `--noprepare` and `--nobuild` flags, so its contents are not executed. This assessment is narrow and limited to the safety of source-fetching and PKGBUILD sourcing. No malicious behavior (e.g., data exfiltration, remote code execution, or unauthorized downloads) occurs during this phase.
</details>
<summary>
No dangerous code executed during source fetch and parse
</summary>
</security_assessment>

+ Initial PKGBUILD audit complete [grey50]-- No dangerous code executed during source fetch and parse[/]

makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare successful

makepkg --printsrcinfo successful
Reviewing required files...
[0/2] Reviewing PKGBUILD...
[0/2] Reviewing PKGBUILD, ttf-iosevka-34.6.3-1-LICENSE.md...
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD retrieves the Iosevka font files and corresponding license from the official GitHub repository of the project, using a versioned release URL and pinned checksums. The source URLs are predictable and directly tied to the declared upstream (https://github.com/be5invis/Iosevka), and the SHA-256 checksums are provided for both the font archive and the license file, ensuring integrity.

The package() function performs standard operations: creating a directory in the package root, installing TTF font files with appropriate permissions, and placing the license in the standard location under /usr/share/licenses. There are no network calls, file modifications outside the package directory, or use of dangerous commands like eval, base64, or curl in the build process.

All operations align with standard AUR packaging practices for a font package. The sources are verifiable, the build is reproducible, and no obfuscated or suspicious code is present. The package does not execute arbitrary code or access sensitive user data.
</details>
<summary>
Standard font package with verified sources
</summary>
</security_assessment>

[1/2] Reviewing ttf-iosevka-34.6.3-1-LICENSE.md...
+ Reviewed PKGBUILD. Status: SAFE -- Standard font package with verified sources
LLM auditresponse for ttf-iosevka-34.6.3-1-LICENSE.md:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The file provided is a LICENSE.md file containing the SIL Open Font License v1.1 and copyright notice for the ttf-iosevka package. It is a standard licensing document that outlines the terms under which the font software may be used, modified, and distributed. There is no executable code, script, or command present in this file. The content is purely legal and descriptive, with no potential for malicious activity.

All text in the file pertains to open-source licensing norms and is consistent with common practice for open font projects. The inclusion of a URL (http://scripts.sil.org/OFL) for the license and FAQ is standard and points to a legitimate, well-known source. No network operations, file modifications, or system interactions are defined or implied within this file.

As this is a static license file with no active functionality or hidden content, it poses no security risk.
</details>
<summary>
License file, no executable code, safe.</summary>
</security_assessment>

LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD only defines static variables and a `package()` function. The `source` array points to official GitHub release assets over HTTPS, with pinned checksums. There is no `pkgver()` function, no top-level command execution, and no code outside of `package()` that would run during `makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare` or `--printsrcinfo`. The request to fetch sources and source the PKGBUILD is inherently safe; no malicious payloads or data exfiltration mechanisms are present.
</details>
<summary>
Safe; no malicious code executes during source fetch.</summary>
</security_assessment>

LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD downloads a specific versioned release from the official GitHub repository (`https://github.com/be5invis/Iosevka/releases/download/v${pkgver}/PkgTTF-Iosevka-${pkgver}.zip`) and a license file from the same repository's raw content. Both sources are pinned by SHA-256 checksums, ensuring integrity and reproducibility. The `package()` function performs standard operations: creating a font directory and installing the `.ttf` files and license. There are no dangerous commands (curl, wget, eval), no obfuscated code, and no exfiltration or backdoor attempts. This is a clean, typical font package.
</details>
<summary>Standard font PKGBUILD with pinned checksums, no malicious content.</summary>
</security_assessment>

data.reasoning: Both models confirmed the PKGBUILD uses verified HTTPS sources with pinned checksums and performs standard font installation. LICENSE.md is non-executable. No build/prepare functions or install hooks exist to analyze.
learnings: File selection: Models correctly prioritized PKGBUILD and license, but audit tool should automatically flag any .install files if present
Assessment quality: Both models demonstrated good understanding of AUR security boundaries (HTTPS+checksums vs runtime execution risks)
Failure modes: No issues here, but models might struggle with packages containing build-time hooks not explicitly called out in prompts
